steps.title: Eliminate by direction
steps.content: The graph falls from left to right, so the slope is negative. Two other options have positive slopes, so eliminate them. Now only C and D are possible.

steps.title: Use the marked points
steps.content: The graph passes through $(0,-5)$ and $(7,-8)$. The slope is $$\frac{-8-(-5)}{7-0}=-\frac{3}{7},$$ and the $y$-intercept is $-5$. Therefore the equation is $f(x)=-\frac{3}{7}x-5$.

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: $f(x)=-\frac{7}{3}x-5$ is too steep because its slope is $-\frac{7}{3}$ instead of $-\frac{3}{7}$. Therefore answer C, $f(x)=-\frac{3}{7}x-5$, is correct.
